brief_justification: Paper A studies quantum many-body dynamics: it uses the semiclassical periodic-orbit framework plus the Bethe-ansatz algebraic structure of integrable spin chains to locate scarred eigenstates and explain tower spacing from decoupling magnon pairs in the Bethe equations. Paper B is an algebraic-geometry/string-theory construction: hyperkahler intermediate Jacobians of a CY3, 3d Rozansky-Witten theories, and an SYZ mirror-symmetry transformation exchanging A- and B-branes in derived categories with varying stability conditions. There is no shared transferable machinery -- periodic-orbit/Bethe-ansatz dynamics versus SYZ mirror-symmetry geometry are governed by entirely different structures, and no coherent role-for-role mapping between their elements holds.
